THOMAS M DIACHENKO

*ADMITTED IN OREGON ONLY

DEC 22 1989

Hon James L Focht
Counsel

TELEPHONE
(619) 557-7600

FAX (619) 557-7606

WRITER'S DIRECT LINE:

557-7606

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for Formal Written Opinion

Pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b) and Regulation
section 18329 (of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations),
request is hereby made for formal written advice.

This request is filed on behalf of Herbert H. Stickney of 173
Morro Hills Road, Fallbrook, California 92028, who has reviewed this
letter and authorized the filing of this request by the undersigned,
Wallace R. Peck, the district counsel for Rainbow Municipal Water
District.

Mr. Stickney is one of five elected members of the board of
directors of Rainbow Municipal Water District, a district formed
pursuant to the Municipal Water District Law of 1911, as amended
(Division 20 of the Water Code). Each director is elected from a
separate division of the district by the voters of that division.
Director Stickney represents Division 2.

The district supplies water service throughout its 77.8 square
mile territory through over 5,000 meters to approximately 12,000
residents. The district supplies sewer service to only a portion of
its territory. About one-third of its water customers receive sewer
service from the district. The remaining rely primarily on private
septic systems.

In response to requests from certain property owners within
Division 2, the board has been asked to consider authorizing the
construction of a sewer line to serve about 85 properties which are
or may be unable to rely upon septic tanks for sewage disposal. This
request has generated considerable controversy, inasmuch as other
residents view the extension of the sewer line as unnecessary and as

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
December 21, 1989
Page 2


a growth inducing facility which would disturb the rural lifestyle of
the community.  Most homes in the area are situated upon lots of over
two acres in size.

     One of the properties that would be served by the new sewer line
would be that of Mr. and Mrs. Ronald P. Thon.  The Thons own a home
situated on a 2.42 acre parcel of land which has been declared by the
County Department of Health Services to be unfit for human habitation
due to a defective private sewage disposal system.  The proposed
district sewer system would allow reoccupation of the house by the
Thons.

     The Thons are personal friends of Director Stickney.  Director
Stickney and his wife own about 5.3 acres of land on which is
situated their residence.  The boundary of their property is
approximately 700 feet from the boundary of the Thons' property, with
the two residences being less than 1,000 feet apart.  The property of
Director Stickney is about 1,200 feet from the proposed location of
the sewer pipeline.

     If a new sewer system is not installed, the value of the Thons
property will be substantially diminished.  Conversely, if it is
installed, its value will increase.  This will have a substantial
effect upon the value of Director Stickney's property.  The estimated
market value of Director Stickney's property is over $750,000.
Although it is difficult to evaluate the financial effect of any
decision, Director Stickney believes that the ultimate effect of the
board's decision upon the fair market value of his property will be
to increase or decrease it by at least $10,000.

     It should be further noted that the Thons have indicated that
they are financially frustrated and would seriously consider letting
their house fall into a state of disrepair if the sewage line is not
constructed.  This would serve as a warning to prospective buyers in
the area of potential septic system failures, which would affect the
value of neighboring properties, including Director Stickney's.

     In addition, Director Stickney's property currently relies upon
a septic tank.  If the new sewer line is constructed, Director
Stickney may be able to utilize it in the future should his septic
system become inadequate, thereby affecting the value of his
property.  At the present time, no such need is apparent.  Moreover,
the board has been asked to limit the properties that would be able
to connect to the new sewer line.  Such a decision could prohibit
Director Stickney from connecting his property to the sewer.

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
December 21, 1989
Page 3


     Based upon the foregoing facts, Director Stickney desires an
opinion as to whether he is disqualified under the Political Reform
Act of 1974 from participating in proceedings of the board of
directors of Rainbow Municipal Water District which will result in a
decision to build or not to build a sewer line that would serve the
area.

     Director Stickney also desires to know if your opinion would be
different if the decision of the board were not to build a public
system, but to allow property owners in the area to construct a
private sewage line, which would connect to the district's system at
a point over one mile away.  The private sewer line would serve the
property of the Thons and could serve the property of Director
Stickney.

     If you desire any further facts or information please do not
hesitate to contact the undersigned or Director Stickney.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        Wallace R. Peck
                                        District Counsel

cc:  Herbert H. Stickney
     Rainbow Municipal Water District

PAULA A. NELSON
THOMAS M. DIACMENKO

*ADMITTED IN OREGON ONLY

SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101

W. H. JENNINGS
1899-1983

HON. JAMES L. FOCHT
OF COUNSEL

TELEPHONE
(619) 557-7600

FAX (619) 557-7760

WRITER'S DIRECT LINE:

557-7606

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Attention: Jeevan Ahuja, Esq.

Re: Request for Formal Written Opinion (Letter No. 89-710)

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

As you requested, enclosed is a map of the district showing the
proposed sewer line and the area known as Rolling Hills that is
proposed to be served which has about 85 residential sites. Also
shown are the property of the Thons and the property of Mr. Stickney.
There are other private properties around and near the area. The
many pipelines shown on the map are waterlines, not sewer lines.

You are correct in your suggestion that there are other houses
and properties outside the service area whose value may be affected
by development or nondevelopment within the service area. Since the
line is intended to obviate a potential health problem and allow the
continued use or development of property within the service area, the
line will affect values outside the service area. Thus, there is
support for the argument that the effect upon Mr. Stickney's property
is similar to the effect upon other properties in the area, and it
may be that the effect on his property will be substantially the same
as upon a significant segment of the public.

Please do not hesitate to call if you need further information
to assist you in making your determination.

Very truly yours,

Wallace R. Peck
District Counsel

cc: Herbert W. Stickney
Rainbow M.W. District

JENNINGS, ENGSTRAND & HENRIKSON
A PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION

501 WEST BROADWAY

SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101

PAUL D. ENGSTRAND

PATRICIA A. GUERNSEY
ROBERT C. HAWKINS
SHELDON A. WILENSKY
D. PETER AVIS
VICTOR MIZRACHI
ROBERT J. HEINO
ROBERT B. MACDONALD
ROBIN TITUS
FELICIA F. BLAKLEY
PAULA A. NELSON
THOMAS M. GACHENRO

W. H. JENNINGS
1899-1983

HON. JAMES L. FOCHT
OF COUNSEL

TELEPHONE
(619) 557-7600

FAX (619) 557-7760

WRITER'S DIRECT LINE:

557-7606

*ADMITTED IN OREGON ONLY

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Attention: Jeevan Ahuja, Esq.

Re: Request for Formal Written Opinion
(Letter No. 89-710)

Dear Mr. Ahuja:

Pursuant to your request, this is to follow up with additional facts regarding the proposed sewer system that is being considered by the Board of Directors of Rainbow Municipal Water District in order to assist in your determination as to whether the board's decision would affect Mr. Stickney differently than it would affect the public generally.

As previously advised, the district supplies sewer service to about one-third of its water customers. In a guest list question there is no sewer service. As part of the district's Master Sewer Plan, dated August, 1985, the district contemplated the construction of an 8-inch or 10-inch sewer trunk line to serve this area, which, at that time, was projected to have between 720 to 1,640 homes, depending upon the density allowed. Subsequent county plans indicate that lots in the area will have a minimum size of two to four acres, which would result, ultimately, in approximately 720 homes.

At the present time, it would not appear that the sewer system contemplated by the master plan will ever be built due to the fact that county planning policy is not to approve new subdivisions that require sewer systems. In the meantime, a small portion of the area is encountering difficulties in the use of septic systems, and, therefore, has requested the installation of a small-diameter

Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 26, 1990
Page Two

pressure pipe system which, by reason of its size, could only serve
approximately 85 homes. Since the proposed system is to be limited
in scope and limited in the area served, it would appear to be more
like a local assessment district, rather than areawide service
affecting the public generally or a significant segment of the
public.

As noted in my letter of December 21, 1989, the proposed
decision of the board could affect Mr. Stickney in two ways:

(1) The value of his residence will be affected by the increase
or decrease in the value of his neighbor's property; and

(2) He may be able to utilize the sewer system in the future.

I hope the foregoing information will assist you in your
analysis as to whether the effect of the proposed decision on Mr.
Stickney will be distinguishable from its effect upon the public
generally or whether the effect will be substantially the same as on
all members of the public or a significant segment of the public.

Finally, I have reviewed the letter to you from Messrs. Wireman
and Chaney, dated January 18, 1990. It merely emphasizes the
community controversy over the issue of a new sewer. I do not
believe it disputes or adds any facts relevant to the legal issues
involved in the request for an opinion.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to
call me.

Very truly yours,

Wallace R. Peck
District Counsel

cc: Herbert H. Stickney
    Rainbow Municipal Water District
